text:
Market today: Mixed and a little jittery. The S&P 500 is about flat, but tech (Nasdaq) slipped again while money rotated into smaller companies. The market is still shaking off last week's Fed-driven dip and is waiting on Thursday's big inflation report (PCE).

Your holdings: Two need a look today, and both are on margin, so the drops sting extra:
- PLTR (down about $796, -6%): it broke below its trend and its average price line, and your scanner flags it as a SELL. Consider trimming or stepping out; if it bounces back toward ~138 that's a nicer spot to sell into.
- AVGO (down about $3,667, -9%): it slipped under its average price line and is losing steam. Consider trimming some, and bail if it drops under 336.
Everything else looks fine: SMH (+19%) and SNOW (+49%) are your winners, and SPY, QQQ, QQQI and CRWD are all holding above their safety lines. SPCX, your brand-new and very volatile SpaceX IPO, is slightly green (+3.7%) but has been sliding a few days in the news, so manage it by hand with a floor around 150.

Buy ideas: Today's strongest setups are mostly more chip names, which you already own a lot of, so adding mostly piles onto the same bet. If you want exposure, AMAT near 623 (bail under 620) had the best risk-reward, and SMH near 662 (bail under 659) just doubles down on a winner you already hold. Micron looks tempting too, but it reports earnings Wednesday, so buying now is a coin-flip on that result.

Bottom line: It's a calm-but-fragile day. Your real decision is what to do about PLTR and AVGO, not chasing a new buy.
